entity.summary: U.S.-based Bitcoin exchange that shut down in April 2013 after losing bank support amid broader U.S. banking and regulatory pressure on Bitcoin businesses.
entity.notes: Death date is month-level because available public reporting places the shutdown in April 2013. Classification uses regulation because reports tie the closure to loss of bank support during a broader U.S. regulatory and banking pullback from Bitcoin exchanges.

events.title: Bitfloor operated as an early U.S. Bitcoin exchange
events.description: Bitfloor operated as a U.S.-based Bitcoin exchange during the early Bitcoin market period.
events.notes: Year-level launch date based on public background references.

events.title: Bitfloor shut down after losing bank support
events.description: Bitfloor shut down in April 2013 after its bank refused to continue doing business with the exchange, leaving it unable to operate normal dollar services.
events.notes: Used as death_date because reporting identifies the April 2013 bank-support loss as the practical end of the exchange.

evidence.title: Bitfloor background reference
evidence.notes: Used only for basic background and original-domain cross-checking.

evidence.title: The Internet Archive Rescues Bitcoiners From Banking Oblivion
evidence.notes: Used for Bitfloor shutdown and bank-support context.

evidence.title: Bitcoin's Big Bank Problem: Why Did Mt. Gox Halt U.S. Payouts?
evidence.notes: Used for U.S. banking and regulatory context and report that Bitfloor was out of business.
